tasks.title: adapters/actorevidence: the read-only per-domain actor-evidence Reader seam + typed Evidence record

scenarios: Actor evidence is collected only by registered read-only readers with least-privilege credentials

tasks.title: core/attribution: deterministic attributor — taxonomy derivation, absence/ambiguity rules, reader-failure honesty

scenarios: Absent evidence yields unattributable and the pre-feature ladder unchanged
A reader error is treated as absent evidence with a recorded warning and never blocks triage
Contradictory non-suspicious evidence escalates to the approver graph with all candidates recorded
Agent free-text can never set or alter the taxonomy value

tasks.title: Rules-as-data: taxonomy to disposition mapping + INV-20 carve-out rows in the versioned ruleset store, fail-closed loader

scenarios: The taxonomy-to-disposition mapping is loadable data with a closed disposition enum
An absent or corrupt mapping escalates every non-unattributable taxonomy to a human
A valid carve-out classifies a harness fault authorized-test, heals, and records the attribution
An expired carve-out rule no longer matches and the attribution stands down

tasks.title: modules/actorevidence/pve: native PVE task-log reader (read-only token, guest resolve, bounded timeout)

scenarios: The PVE task-log reader returns actor evidence for a guest lifecycle change
The PVE reader authenticates with a read-only token distinct from the actuation token

tasks.title: Runner wiring: attribute activity, GatedInput disposition fields, attributed-self already-remediated terminal outcome

scenarios: An admin-attributed fault stands down to the approver graph and is never auto-undone
A TG-self-remediated incident terminates already-remediated without re-actuation
An authorized-test outcome traverses every existing gate unchanged

tasks.title: Persistence + tracer surfacing: migration 0034 (session_triage attribution column + evidence blob), ledger signals, tracer attribute step

scenarios: The attribution outcome and evidence references persist on session_triage and the governance ledger
The decision tracer surfaces the attribution as a named trace step
Persisted evidence is retention-bounded and redacted at every boundary

tasks.title: Security-escalation routing: suspicious disposition to POLL_PAUSE + security channel notification (Phase 2)

scenarios: An unsanctioned-actor change polls with security escalation and is never auto-healed
A covered audit trail missing an entry for an observed mutation escalates as suspicious

tasks.title: modules/actorevidence/journal: systemd-journal / sudo / auditd action-evidence reader (Phase 2)

scenarios: The journal reader returns typed actor evidence for a privileged host action
The journal reader fails closed when no known-hosts file is configured

tasks.notes: Reuses syslogng.NewNativeRunner (native host-key-verified read-only SSH, distroless-safe) + credential.IdentityResolver per-host identity, mirroring modules/observability/hostdiag. Deterministic JSON parse (journalctl -o json / ausearch) into []attribution.Evidence — never raw text to the model.

scenarios: The k8s audit reader returns actor evidence for a cluster object change

tasks.title: modules/actorevidence/netbox + awx: CMDB changelog and job-history readers (Phase 3)

scenarios: The NetBox changelog reader returns actor evidence for an inventory change
The AWX job-history reader returns actor evidence for a playbook-driven change

tasks.title: modules/actorevidence/gitopsmr: GitOps MR-history reader (Phase 3; docker dropped — see note)

scenarios: The GitOps MR-history reader returns actor evidence for a declarative change

tasks.note: docker-events reader DROPPED (grounded 2026-07-24): the docker daemon records no human actor (no audit shim on the socket; container lifecycle events carry the runtime, not the invoking user), so a docker attribution reader would be inert. Shipping an inert reader violates the evidence-gated-honesty invariant (REQ-2307); the GitOps MR reader already attributes the declarative change that a docker action merely enacts.

tasks.title: Identity/auth enrichment seam: SanctionResolver + LDAP/FreeIPA resolver + AttributeActivity dynamic-sanction fold

scenarios: An identity fact can never by itself set a taxonomy value
Identity enrichment leaves the deterministic core attributor unchanged
A confirmed live admin is promoted to a stand-down
A disabled admin credential in use is demoted to a security escalation
A directory outage reproduces the Phase-1 classification
Identity data is retention-bounded and redacted at every boundary
A missing authentication event never moves a classification

tasks.notes: SanctionResolver is a NON-Reader seam (never mints taxonomy). The LDAP resolver reuses modules/credsource/ldap transport (go-ldap/v3, LDAPS CA-verified, NL->GR failover) + one new single-principal query (nsAccountLock/krbPrincipalExpiration/memberOf/objectClass). The fold runs in AttributeActivity over a COPIED cfg.Sanctioned: ADD confirmed live admins (promotion), REMOVE disabled static admins (demotion). core/attribution/attribution.go stays byte-for-byte unchanged. SanctionedGroups is loadable rules-as-data. Fail-open-to-static, positive-evidence-only. RADIUS (REQ-2321) is advisory-annotation-only, NOT built here.
